Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search Airtable for Lily Marchetti relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Lily Marchetti") or airtable_list_records with manual filtering. Find TWO conflicting records. Record rec_reloc_marchetti has Status "Cancelled" (modified 2026-02-10, notes confirm employee decided not to relocate, cancellation sent to Jake Loomis at Heartland on 2/10, no acknowledgment received, Vendor Cost $0). Record recReloc00000002 has Status "Completed" with move dates April 25-26, Nashville to Chicago, special requirements noting indoor cat (8 lbs) and oversized iMac. The "Completed" record was created on 2026-04-01, months after the February cancellation. These records directly contradict each other.
Search Linear for Lily Marchetti tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "Marchetti" or "Lily") or searching within project proj_canopy. Find THREE orphaned tickets still assigned to Blessing Okafor for a move that was cancelled. Ticket #304 "Apartment Search - Lily Marchetti - Chicago (Canopy Health)" with state_id state_in_progress, due 2026-04-17 (overdue), describing a pet-friendly 1BR apartment search with $1,800-$2,300 budget. Ticket #305 "Flight Booking - Lily Marchetti - BNA to ORD (Canopy Health)" with state_id state_todo, due 2026-04-18, noting cat will travel in cabin. Ticket #306 "Moving Coordination - Lily Marchetti - Nashville to Chicago (Canopy Health)" with state_id state_todo, due 2026-04-20, listing Heartland Movers as vendor with oversized iMac as special handling. All three tickets were created 2026-04-01, the same day as the erroneous "Completed" Airtable record.
Search emails for the Marchetti cancellation paper trail using search_emails (query: "Marchetti cancellation" or "Marchetti Heartland" or "Jake Loomis cancellation"). Also search the SENT folder. Find evidence that Chloe Vance sent the cancellation notice to Jake Loomis at Heartland on February 10, 2026, and that Heartland never acknowledged or responded to it. This establishes what was communicated and when and confirms that Heartland had no basis for billing the move.
Search Linear for the Heartland Q1 billing dispute using linear_list_issues (query: "Heartland" or "vendor invoice reconciliation"). Find Ticket #342 "Vendor invoice reconciliation - Heartland Movers Q1 2026 (HM-2026-Q1-0042)" assigned to Chloe Vance, documenting that Heartland submitted a Q1 invoice billing for 8 moves at $1,600 each ($12,800 total), but only 5 of 8 are legitimate, resulting in $4,800 overbilling. The Marchetti Nashville-to-Chicago move is listed as Disputed Item #1 ($1,600) with evidence that the cancellation email was sent to Jake Loomis on Feb 10 with no acknowledgment.
Search Airtable for Ines Kovac relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Name", value: "Ines Kovac"). Find TWO records with conflicting vendor information. Record recReloc00000007 has Status "Completed", Brooklyn to Austin, move dates April 22-24, Special Requirements noting 5th-floor walkup, no elevator, 4-person crew needed, walkup surcharge ($350), heavy items including 6-7 boxes of books, large iMac, Wacom Cintiq drawing tablet. Record rec_reloc_kovac has Status "Completed", Brooklyn to Austin, but lists Moving Vendor as "Heartland Movers" with Vendor Cost "$1600" even though the Linear tickets (#319, #320, #321) and QuickBooks bill SR-2026-0422 all reference Swift Relocations as the actual vendor. This vendor mismatch is the same pattern as the Priya Venkatesh entry in the Heartland billing dispute where the move was completed by Swift but Heartland billed for it.
Search Linear for Ines Kovac tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "Kovac" or "Ines" or "PivotPoint"). Find Tickets #319 (Apartment Search, state_done), #320 (Flight Booking JFK to AUS, state_done), and #321 (Moving Coordination Brooklyn to Austin, state_done, vendor: Swift Relocations, notes: "5th-floor walk-up, no elevator, expect walk-up surcharge from mover") all completed and assigned to Blessing Okafor. The vendor listed in the Linear tickets (Swift Relocations) contradicts the vendor in Airtable record rec_reloc_kovac (Heartland Movers at $1,600).
Search Slack channels #operations (C006) and #finance (C005) using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Heartland" or "billing" or "dispute" or "Q1 invoice") to find internal discussions about the Heartland billing dispute and verify the scope of the overbilling. Find context confirming the $4,800 overbilling (3 disputed charges) already documented. Cross-referencing the Kovac vendor mismatch against the dispute reveals that if Heartland also billed $1,600 for the Kovac move but Swift actually handled it, this is potentially a fourth disputed charge, increasing the total disputed amount from $4,800 to $6,400.
Search Airtable for all relocation records assigned to Blessing Okafor using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Assigned Coordinator", value: "Blessing Okafor") or airtable_list_records with manual filtering. Find at least five overdue records where the move end dates have already passed (relative to universe date April 26, 2026) but the status still shows "In Progress": Kevin Okafor (recReloc00000000030, Canopy Health, Phoenix to Portland, dates April 14-16), Preethi Nair (recReloc00000000036, BrightLoop Analytics, Atlanta to Raleigh, dates April 21-23), Marcus Webb (recMarcusWebbDetroitChicago, Canopy Health, Detroit to Chicago, dates April 10-12), Raj Patel (recRajPatelChicagoDenver, NorthWind Technologies, Chicago to Denver, dates April 21-23), and Emilia Cruz (recEmiliaCruzChicagoDenver, NorthWind Technologies, Chicago to Denver, dates April 14-18). All five have move end dates before April 26 but remain in active status.
Search QuickBooks for the Heartland Q1 invoice using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria: [{field: "VendorRef", value: "Heartland"}]) or quickbooks_get_bill. Find TWO bill records. First is bill_heartland_q1_2026 (DocNumber: HM-2026-Q1-0042), the original invoice for $12,800 total (8 line items at $1,600 each), Balance $12,800 (unpaid), due April 25. Second is bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold (DocNumber: HM-2026-Q1-0042-HOLD), the same invoice with a payment hold applied. Line items 6-8 are marked "DISPUTED" for Lily Marchetti (cancelled move), Danielle Osei (cancelled move), and Priya Venkatesh (completed by Swift not Heartland). Critically, line item 3 (Ines Kovac, Brooklyn to Austin, $1,600) is marked "[VERIFIED]", meaning Chloe's team accepted it as a legitimate Heartland charge. But the agent should recognize from the Kovac investigation that Swift actually handled the move, making this verification incorrect and Kovac a possible fourth disputed charge. The full 8-line analysis should cross-reference each billed move against Airtable records to determine which charges are legitimate and which are not.
Update the Lily Marchetti Airtable records to reflect reality using airtable_update_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id: "tblRelocations01"). The erroneous "Completed" record recReloc00000002 should be corrected by updating its status to "Cancelled" to match rec_reloc_marchetti, or deleted via airtable_delete_records if it was created in error. The goal is to eliminate the conflicting status that contributed to Heartland billing for a cancelled move.
Close or cancel the three orphaned Marchetti Linear tickets using linear_update_issue. Tickets #304 (issue_canopy_marchetti_apt), #305 (issue_canopy_marchetti_flight), and #306 (issue_canopy_marchetti_movers) should have their state updated to done (state_id: "state_done") with a note explaining the move was cancelled in February. These tickets should not remain in active or todo states for a move that is not happening.
Correct the Kovac vendor mismatch in Airtable using airtable_update_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id: "tblRelocations01"). Record rec_reloc_kovac incorrectly lists Heartland Movers as the vendor when Swift Relocations actually handled the move (as confirmed by Linear tickets #319-321 and QuickBooks bill SR-2026-0422). Update the record to reflect the correct vendor assignment. This correction is important both for data integrity and for the ongoing Heartland billing dispute.
Update any obviously stale overdue records in Blessing's portfolio using airtable_update_records. At minimum, records like Kevin Okafor and Preethi Nair where the move dates have clearly passed and there are no complicating factors should have their status changed from "In Progress" to "Completed" or otherwise updated to reflect that the move window has closed.
Look up contact details for Marcus Thorne and Hana Kim using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus Thorne") and contacts_search_contacts (query: "Hana Kim"). Find marcus.thorne@moveops.com (Head of Finance) and hana.kim@moveops.com (Accountant). These are needed for the email or Slack notification about the Heartland situation.
Post a summary in Slack #operations (channel_id: C006) using conversations_add_message from Blessing's perspective (user_id: moveops_blessing_okafor). The message should cover what was found (Marchetti dual records and orphaned tickets, Kovac vendor mismatch, overdue records across the portfolio) and what was fixed (records corrected, tickets closed, vendor assignments updated). This addresses Blessing's request to drop a note in ops so Chloe knows she is on it.
Flag findings relevant to the Heartland billing dispute to Marcus Thorne and Hana Kim. Send via email (send_email from blessing.okafor@moveops.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com and hana.kim@moveops.com) or via Slack DM or post in #finance (channel_id: C005). The message should flag that the Marchetti cancelled-move records have been cleaned up, confirming the $1,600 Marchetti charge on the Heartland Q1 invoice is indeed invalid. It should also flag the Kovac vendor mismatch discovery where Airtable showed Heartland as vendor at $1,600 but Swift actually handled the move, which may represent an additional disputed charge not yet captured in the $4,800 overbilling total. Finally, it should note that the QuickBooks HOLD bill currently marks the Kovac line as "[VERIFIED]" but this verification appears incorrect given the Swift discovery.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The erroneous Lily Marchetti Airtable record (recReloc00000002, which incorrectly showed Status "Completed") was updated to "Cancelled" (via airtable_update_records) or deleted (via airtable_delete_records).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The trajectory clearly shows an airtable_update_records call targeting recReloc00000002 with Status set to 'Cancelled', along with an annotation documenting the correction. This directly addresses the erroneous 'Completed' record for the cancelled Marchetti move.
✓Run #2The agent called airtable_update_records targeting recReloc00000002 with Status set to 'Cancelled'. Confirmed by the tool result showing the record was updated to Cancelled status.
✓Run #3The agent called airtable_update_records targeting recReloc00000002 with Status set to 'Cancelled' and a detailed correction note. The tool result confirms the update was successful.
